attacking_style: Explosive accelerations on steep gradients (14%+) in final 1-2km, often bridging to breakaways rather than initiating attacks. Prefers finishing punches over sustained efforts.
team_tactics: Operates with tactical freedom separate from team GC objectives, hunting stage wins from breakaway positions rather than supporting team leadership.
terrain_preference: Excels on extremely steep finishing climbs (14%+ gradients) where power-to-weight ratios are decisive. Performs well from breakaway scenarios requiring late-race bridging efforts.
vulnerability: Susceptible to being dropped on initial explosive attacks by pure climbers (Kuss example), suggesting weakness against sustained high-intensity accelerations early in climbs.
key_patterns: Finishes second stealthily without visible struggle, appearing fresher than result suggests (2 instances)
Bridges gaps on steep gradients then immediately attacks, superior late-race acceleration (2 instances)
Operates as stage hunter independent of GC ambitions, breakaway specialist approach (3 instances)
Shows smart pacing recovery after initial drops, tactical maturity over raw power (1 direct instance)
form_trajectory: positive: 2022 results show consistent stealth podiums and tactical awareness, maintaining competitive edge 4 years beyond 2018 Vuelta stage win
